goal: Prove that Cortex has a working OpenSearch backend whose schema and behavior are compatible with the MVP retrieval model.
preconditions: The service is deployable through the Moltbox service model.
Operator access to gateway lifecycle commands is available.
The target host has the intended storage devices or an explicitly documented substitute.

tests.title: Service deploy, status, and bootstrap states
tests.objective: Verify gateway deployment succeeds and Cortex can distinguish service health from bootstrap state.
preconditions: Service metadata and compose templates exist.
procedure: Run the standard service deploy flow.
Run the standard service status flow.
Check cluster reachability and readiness.
Differentiate unreachable, reachable-but-unbootstrapped, and bootstrapped states.
expected_results: Deploy succeeds through the standard control plane.
Status exposes a healthy or inspectable service state.
Bootstrap state can be distinguished from raw service health.
failure_conditions: Deploy fails through the standard control plane.
Status does not expose a usable service state.
Bootstrap and service health remain conflated.

tests.title: Schema compatibility and Cortex bootstrap
tests.objective: Verify Cortex-owned indexes and aliases can be created with the intended mapping shape.
procedure: Run the Cortex bootstrap path.
Inspect the knowledge and retrieval-trace mappings.
Inspect the versioned indexes and stable aliases.
expected_results: Knowledge and retrieval-trace mappings are created successfully.
Governance, typed-signal, provenance, link, and vector-capable fields are accepted.
Stable aliases resolve to versioned indexes.
failure_conditions: Bootstrap fails or is not idempotent.
Aliases or versioned indexes are missing.

tests.title: CLI and plugin posture
tests.objective: Verify the operator can reach native inspection surfaces and that required plugins are present.
procedure: Run `moltbox opensearch --version`.
Inspect plugin inventory through the documented operator path.
Capture the exact command path used for version, health, and snapshot inspection.
expected_results: Native CLI passthrough is reachable.
Required plugins are present.
Operator command path is explicit and reusable.
failure_conditions: CLI passthrough is missing or undocumented.
No documented command path exists for routine inspection.

tests.title: Resource, storage, and security posture
tests.objective: Verify the service is running with the intended RAM, CPU, mount, and security posture.
procedure: Inspect rendered service config or live container state.
Confirm the configured memory, CPU, and no-GPU posture.
Confirm the NVMe data path and backup-HDD snapshot path.
Confirm Security plugin enablement and credential bootstrap path.
expected_results: Service matches the intended 64 GiB memory and 4-core posture.
Data and backup paths resolve correctly.
Security is enabled and secret injection is explicit.
failure_conditions: Live posture drifts from the intended resource allocation.
Mount paths are incorrect or missing.
required_evidence: Rendered config or live inspection output.

tests.title: Snapshot repository and nightly backup policy
tests.objective: Verify backup configuration is active and verifiable.
procedure: Verify `path.repo` and mounted snapshot path.
Register the filesystem snapshot repository.
Run repository verification.
Verify or create the nightly snapshot-management policy with explicit retention.
expected_results: Snapshot repository verifies successfully.
Nightly snapshot policy exists with retention.

tests.title: Service account, smoke index, and snapshot restore
tests.objective: Verify security and backup posture are usable in practice through smoke operations.
preconditions: OS-REG-003, OS-REG-004, and OS-REG-005 have passed.
procedure: Authenticate with the admin or operator credential.
Provision or verify the Cortex service account and role.
Create a temporary `cortex-smoke-<id>` index and sentinel document.
Run one smoke snapshot and one throwaway restore drill.
Delete the original and restored smoke targets after verification.
expected_results: Cortex service account authenticates successfully.
Temporary smoke index lifecycle succeeds.
Snapshot and restore drill both succeed.

tests.title: Write, read, provenance, and governance query behavior
tests.objective: Verify Cortex-shaped documents can be indexed and queried safely.
procedure: Index a small sample knowledge set by stable ID.
Upsert one sample document by the same stable ID.
Run a governance-filtered lexical query.
Fetch the winning document by ID.
Query provenance by `artifact_id` and read back a trace sample.
expected_results: Stable-ID upsert works without duplicate ambiguity.
Governance filters exclude wrong-scope documents before ranking matters.
Knowledge document, provenance, and trace are all inspectable.
failure_conditions: ID, provenance, or trace lookups fail.

tests.title: Diagnostics and operator inspection surfaces
tests.objective: Verify operators can inspect the state needed to debug deployment, mapping, query, and snapshot issues.
procedure: Capture deploy and status output.
Inspect aliases, mappings, snapshot state, and raw query responses.
Capture the exact operator path used for those inspections.
expected_results: Inspection surfaces are available for deploy, aliases, mappings, snapshot state, and queries.
Operator command path is explicit enough to reuse during later epics.
failure_conditions: Operator path for routine inspections remains undocumented.

exit_criteria: All active OpenSearch dependency regression tests pass or have an explicitly approved exception.
Required evidence exists for each regression test.
Later Cortex epics can assume the service exists without additional setup explanation.
